query: What is the current level of crude oil in the US Strategic Petroleum Reserve as of June 2026, per EIA weekly data (WCSSTUS1)? What has been the weekly trajectory over the past 3-6 months (fills or releases)?
answer:
I've completed the research. Summary of findings:

**Current SPR level:** ~325.7 million barrels (325,655 thousand bbl) for the week ending June 26, 2026 per EIA series WCSSTUS1 — down ~1.67% week-over-week and ~19.1% year-over-year.

**Trajectory (past 3–6 months):** The SPR held roughly flat near its 2026 peak of ~415.4M bbl from mid-February through mid-March, then began a historically unprecedented drawdown starting March 27, 2026. It fell ~89.8M barrels over ~14 weeks, with two consecutive all-time weekly release records (8.6M bbl week ending May 8, then 9.92M bbl week ending May 15). Recent weekly draws run ~5.5M–9.9M bbl.

**Driver:** A March 2026 authorization to release 172M barrels (~40% of available stockpile) in response to a Middle East conflict / Strait of Hormuz disruption, coordinated with an IEA ~400M-barrel global release.

sources.snippet: 325.66M bbl for week of Jun 26 2026, down 1.67% from last week and 19.10% from one year ago.

sources.title: DOE has released 17.5 million barrels from the Strategic Petroleum Reserve since March
sources.snippet: 17.5M bbl released March 20-April 24; 7.1M in week ending April 24; SPR at 397.9M bbl; part of 172M-barrel drawdown coordinated with IEA 400M-barrel release addressing Middle East conflict.

sources.title: One of Trump's key oil market fixes is about to break
sources.snippet: Trump administration authorized 172M barrels (~40% of available stockpile) released after Strait of Hormuz closure, coordinated internationally.

sources.title: The 2026 SPR Drawdown Crisis: Two All-Time Weekly Records in Two Weeks
sources.snippet: May 20 report showed 9.92M bbl released for week ending May 15; May 13 report showed 8.6M bbl; prior record 8.4M (Sept 2022).

sources.title: Strategic Petroleum Reserve | Department of Energy
sources.snippet: SPR authorized storage capacity of 714 million barrels across four Gulf Coast sites.
